

Bitencourt, C. & Rapini, A. (2013) [Centres of endemism in the espinhaço
range: Identifying cradles and museums of asclepiadoideae
(apocynaceae)](https://doi.org/10.1080/14772000.2013.865681).
*Systematics and Biodiversity*, **11**, 525--536.

Di Virgilio, G., Laffan, S.W. & Ebach, M.C. (2013) [Quantifying high
resolution transitional breaks in plant and mammal distributions at
regional extent and their association with climate, topography and
geology](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0059227). *PLoS ONE*,
**8**, 59227.

González‐Orozco, C.E., Laffan, S.W., Knerr, N. & Miller, J.T. (2013) [A
biogeographical regionalization of australian acacia
species](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.12153). *Journal of Biogeography*,
**40**, 2156--2166.

González‐Orozco, C.E., Thornhill, A.H., Knerr, N., Laffan, S. & Miller,
J.T. (2013) [Biogeographical regions and phytogeography of the
eucalypts](https://doi.org/10.1111/ddi.12129). *Diversity and
Distributions*, **20**, 46--58.

Kooyman, R.M., Rossetto, M., Sauquet, H. & Laffan, S.W. (2013)
[Landscape patterns in rainforest phylogenetic signal: Isolated islands
of refugia or structured continental
distributions?](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0080685) *PLoS
ONE*, **8**, 80685.

Rosauer, D.F., Ferrier, S., Williams, K.J., Manion, G., Keogh, J.S. &
Laffan, S.W. (2013) [Phylogenetic generalised dissimilarity modelling: A
new approach to analysing and predicting spatial turnover in the
phylogenetic composition of
communities](https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1600-0587.2013.00466.x).
*Ecography*, **37**, 21--32.

Weber, L.C., VanDerWal, J., Schmidt, S., McDonald, W.J.F. & Shoo, L.P.
(2013) [Patterns of rain forest plant endemism in subtropical australia
relate to stable mesic refugia and species dispersal
limitations](https://doi.org/10.1111/jbi.12219). *Journal of
Biogeography*, **41**, 222--238.
